sentence_reviews.text: Content- or address-based operations store and retrieve structured state inside a differentiable model.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning supports broad recurrent, memory, and representation-learning background.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is consistent with differentiable memory systems, but a neural Turing machine/memory-network source would be needed for verified.

sentence_reviews.text: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Recurrence & feedback state, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Differentiable memory read/write is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recurrence & feedback state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning supports recurrent state and gated recurrent network background.
sentence_reviews.notes: The relationship is defensible for internal stateful memory mechanisms.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Differentiable memory read/write is modeled as depending on Recurrence & feedback state, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as LSTMs & GRUs, xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids, Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
